nodes.title: Jomon Period Begins
nodes.description: Japan's earliest distinct culture emerges, characterized by hunter-gatherer lifestyles and pottery decorated with cord patterns.

nodes.title: Yayoi Period Begins
nodes.description: Rice cultivation and iron/bronze metallurgy are introduced to Japan, transitioning it from hunter-gatherer to agricultural society.

nodes.title: Introduction of Buddhism
nodes.description: Buddhism is officially introduced to Japan from Baekje, heavily shaping subsequent culture and political systems.

nodes.title: Prince Shotoku's Regency
nodes.description: Prince Shotoku becomes regent, initiating active adoption of Chinese culture and Buddhism.

nodes.title: Prince Shotoku's Constitution
nodes.description: Prince Shotoku enacts the Seventeen-Article Constitution, establishing Buddhist and Confucian moral values in government.

nodes.title: Taika Reforms
nodes.description: A series of political doctrines established after the fall of the Soga clan, creating a centralized government based on the Chinese model.

nodes.title: Nara Period Begins
nodes.description: Heijo-kyo (Nara) is established as the permanent capital, starting a cultural peak.

nodes.title: Kojiki & Nihon Shoki Compiled
nodes.description: The first historical records and mythologies of Japan are compiled, establishing imperial genealogy and early myths.

nodes.title: Manyoshu Compiled
nodes.description: The oldest existing collection of Japanese poetry (waka) is completed, containing over 4,500 poems.

nodes.title: Heian Period
nodes.description: The capital moves to Heian-kyo (Kyoto), initiating a classical golden age of art and literature.

nodes.title: The Tale of Genji Written
nodes.description: Murasaki Shikibu writes what is widely considered the world's first novel, capturing the peak of Heian court culture.

nodes.title: Kamakura Shogunate
nodes.description: Minamoto no Yoritomo establishes the military dictatorship, beginning the feudal era of shoguns.

nodes.title: Mongol Invasions of Japan
nodes.description: Japan repels major invasion attempts by the Mongol Empire, aided by typhoons later named Kamikaze.

nodes.title: Kenmu Restoration
nodes.description: Emperor Go-Daigo overthrows the Kamakura Shogunate, briefly restoring imperial rule before the Muromachi Shogunate begins.

nodes.title: Muromachi Shogunate
nodes.description: Ashikaga Takauji establishes the shogunate in Kyoto, bringing cultural growth alongside rising regional lord power.

nodes.title: Onin War
nodes.description: A civil war over shogunal succession devastates Kyoto and initiates the Sengoku (Warring States) Period.

nodes.title: Sengoku Period
nodes.description: The Onin War triggers a century of civil war and social upheaval among warlords.

nodes.title: First European Contact
nodes.description: Portuguese traders arrive on Tanegashima, introducing matchlock firearms which revolutionize Japanese warfare.

nodes.title: Introduction of Christianity
nodes.description: Francis Xavier, a Jesuit missionary, arrives in Kagoshima, introducing Christianity to Japan.

nodes.title: Unification of Japan
nodes.description: Toyotomi Hideyoshi completes the unification of Japan, ending the chaotic Sengoku period.

nodes.title: Battle of Sekigahara
nodes.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu defeats rival western clans, clearing the path to establish the Tokugawa Shogunate.

nodes.title: Tokugawa Shogunate
nodes.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu unifies Japan, establishing the Edo period of long peace and isolation.

nodes.title: Sakoku Isolation Decreed
nodes.description: The shogunate strictly prohibits travel abroad and closes borders to foreigners to maintain social order and stability.

nodes.title: Genroku Edo Culture
nodes.description: A boom in urban merchant culture yields Ukiyo-e woodblock prints, Kabuki, and Haiku poetry.

nodes.title: Perry's Black Ships Arrive
nodes.description: Commodore Matthew Perry's U.S. Navy fleet enters Tokyo Bay, forcing Japan to end its two centuries of isolation (Sakoku).

nodes.title: Meiji Restoration
nodes.description: Imperial rule is restored, ending the shogunates and launching rapid westernized modernization.

nodes.title: Satsuma Rebellion
nodes.description: Disaffected samurai led by Saigo Takamori rise against the Meiji government in the last major armed uprising.

nodes.title: Meiji Constitution Promulgated
nodes.description: Japan adopts its first modern constitution, establishing a constitutional monarchy and parliament (Imperial Diet).

nodes.title: First Sino-Japanese War
nodes.description: Japan's modernized military defeats Qing Dynasty China, shifting East Asian dominance.

nodes.title: Russo-Japanese War
nodes.description: Japan's victory over the Russian Empire marks the first modern defeat of a European power by an Asian nation.

nodes.title: Japan-Korea Annexation Treaty
nodes.description: Japan formally annexes the Korean Empire, beginning a 35-year period of colonial rule.

nodes.title: Taisho Democracy
nodes.description: An era of political liberalization and cultural internationalism, marking Japan's first party cabinets.

nodes.title: Great Kanto Earthquake
nodes.description: A massive earthquake devastates Tokyo and Yokohama, causing widespread economic shock and political shift.

nodes.title: Showa Era Begins
nodes.description: Hirohito becomes Emperor, marking an era covering military expansionism and later democratic recovery.

nodes.title: Manchurian Incident
nodes.description: The Kwantung Army stages a bombing to justify invading Manchuria, paving the way for military control over foreign policy.

nodes.title: Second Sino-Japanese War
nodes.description: Conflict breaks out between Japan and China following the Marco Polo Bridge Incident, leading to full-scale warfare in Asia.

nodes.title: WWII Pacific War Begins
nodes.description: Japan launches a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, triggering full scale entry into World War II.

nodes.title: Atomic Bombings
nodes.description: The United States drops atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki, leading to Japan's surrender.

nodes.title: Economic Miracle
nodes.description: Post-war Japan achieves rapid industrial and technological growth to become a global economy.

nodes.title: San Francisco Peace Treaty Signed
nodes.description: Japan signs the peace treaty with Allied powers, officially ending WWII hostilities and restoring Japanese sovereignty.

nodes.title: Minamata Disease Recognized
nodes.description: A neurological syndrome caused by severe mercury poisoning is officially recognized in Minamata, leading to modern pollution regulations.

nodes.title: Tokyo Summer Olympics
nodes.description: Japan hosts the Olympics, symbolizing its peaceful return to the international community.

nodes.title: Asset Bubble Peaks
nodes.description: Japan's real estate and stock market bubble peaks, followed by the economic stagnation of the Lost Decades.

nodes.title: Great Hanshin Earthquake & Subway Sarin Attack
nodes.description: The devastating Kobe earthquake and the Aum Shinrikyo sarin gas attack in Tokyo shock the nation's security.

nodes.title: Great East Japan Earthquake
nodes.description: A massive 9.0 magnitude earthquake and devastating tsunami strike northeastern Japan, triggering the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster.

nodes.title: Reiwa Era Begins
nodes.description: Emperor Naruhito ascends the throne following the abdication of Emperor Akihito, marking the start of the Reiwa period.
